channel.title: KillBait RSS Feed
channel.description: KillBait - News highlights delivered clearly and responsibly—no clickbait, no sensationalism

item.title: Biden Administration Mulls Tariffs, Sparking Trade Concerns with Canada
item.description: Concerns are mounting regarding the future of Canada-U.S. trade relations as the Biden administration explores potential tariffs and investigates vulnerabilities in North American supply chains. Sever...

item.title: Body of Teen Who Drowned in River Yala Recovered After Search in Vihiga
item.description: A family in Vihiga County is mourning after the body of a young man, Bramwel Musalia, who drowned in River Yala, was finally retrieved. The recovery brought an end to a tense and emotional search that...

item.title: Affidavits challenge procedure behind Mabuyane’s deregistration at Fort Hare
item.description: Fresh developments have emerged in Eastern Cape Premier Oscar Mabuyane’s ongoing legal challenge against his 2021 deregistration from a master’s degree programme at the University of Fort Hare. Two fo...

item.title: Tanzania and Belarus plan new partnerships in agriculture and digital technology
item.description: Tanzania and Belarus are setting up fresh opportunities for trade, investment, and technology partnerships. A meeting organised by TanTrade on April 28, 2026, brought together business leaders and off...

item.title: Nigeria's Power Sector Grapples with Reform and Fragility
item.description: Nigeria's electricity sector remains in a state of ongoing reform and fragility, as highlighted in a recent assessment. While the government emphasizes progress and reform gains, persistent challenges...

item.title: NU Nazareth and UST advance to SGVIL Final 4
item.description: National University Nazareth School has secured a place in the Final 4 of the Shakey’s Girls Volleyball Invitational League after surviving a tough challenge from St. John’s Institute. In a tightly fo...

item.title: Weak enforcement allows banned reusable syringes to circulate across Pakistan
item.description: An investigative report has revealed that banned reusable syringes are still being widely manufactured, supplied, and used across Pakistan, despite a nationwide prohibition imposed in 2021. The situat...

item.title: Indigenous leaders criticise Anzac Day disruptions and defend Welcome to Country practices
item.description: Indigenous leaders across Australia have strongly criticised incidents of jeering during Welcome to Country ceremonies at Anzac Day dawn services in Melbourne, Sydney and Perth, describing the behavio...

item.title: Palace clarifies King’s stance after Trump links him to Iran nuclear policy
item.description: Buckingham Palace has issued a measured response after US President Donald Trump suggested that King Charles III supports his position that Iran should never obtain nuclear weapons. Speaking at a Whit...

item.title: Barnes drives Raptors as series tied 2-2 heading into Game 5 against Cavaliers
item.description: The Toronto Raptors head into a crucial Game 5 against the Cleveland Cavaliers with momentum after evening their first-round NBA playoff series at two games apiece. Following two difficult losses on t...

item.title: Nara Lokesh takes oath as TDP National Working President, Naidu administers oath to new party committees
item.description: In a significant development within the Telugu Desam Party (TDP), IT and HRD Minister Nara Lokesh was sworn in as the national working president of the party on April 29, 2026. The oath was administer...

item.title: Proposed law seeks to bar former governors from elective seats for five years to allow audits
item.description: In Kenya, a new constitutional amendment proposal is seeking to block former county governors from contesting for parliamentary or county assembly seats for a period of five years after leaving office...

item.title: Epson launches new LifeStudio projectors, EcoTank printers and Creative Corner platform in South Africa
item.description: Epson has introduced a new range of products aimed at enhancing creativity, home entertainment and business productivity in South Africa. The highlight is the LifeStudio projector series, which includ...

item.title: UK Army cautiously restarts Ajax vehicle trials after safety concerns investigation
item.description: The British Army is set to cautiously resume trials of its Ajax armoured vehicles following a pause triggered by reports of illness among soldiers. Testing was halted in November after around 30 perso...

item.title: House Justice Committee Unanimously Finds Probable Cause to Impeach Vice President Sara Duterte
item.description: The House committee on justice unanimously voted on April 29, 2026 to find probable cause to impeach Vice President Sara Duterte. With 53 members present and no objections, the panel determined that t...

item.title: President Zardari visits Mao Zedong's hometown in Shaoshan and arrives in Sanya for CPEC talks and submarine commissioning
item.description: President Asif Ali Zardari, during his official visit to China, went to Shaoshan, the hometown of Chairman Mao Zedong, the founder of the People's Republic of China. There, he laid a wreath at the Sta...

item.title: Science-Backed Tips for Managing Hay Fever and Seasonal Allergies
item.description: Hay fever, or allergic rhinitis, affects around 400 million people worldwide, causing symptoms like sneezing, runny nose, and itchy eyes. The article explains that both the prevalence and severity of ...

item.title: NT Police find child's underwear and clothing items during search for missing five-year-old Sharon Granites in Alice Springs
item.description: In today's episode of The Nightly's Newsworthy segment, host Ben O'Shea discusses several current affairs stories making headlines across Australia. The main focus is the ongoing search for five-year-...

item.title: Red Sox Revamp Coaching Staff with Internal Promotions After Firing Alex Cora and Six Coaches
item.description: The Boston Red Sox continued rebuilding their coaching staff on the second day under interim manager Chad Tracy following the dismissal of manager Alex Cora and six members of his coaching staff. The ...

item.title: FSSAI drafts rules to ban plastic packaging for pan masala, gutka and tobacco products
item.description: The Food Safety and Standards Authority of India (FSSAI) has released a draft amendment to the Food Safety and Standards (Packaging) Regulations, 2018, proposing a complete ban on plastic packaging fo...
